definition: Define an interface for creating objects, but let subclasses decide which concrete class to instantiate.
ruleOfThumb: When object creation logic keeps branching by type, move creation behind a factory method.

citation.author: Gamma, Helm, Johnson & Vlissides (GoF)
citation.bookTitle: Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software

questions.prompt: What maintenance risk shows up as this notification list grows?

options.text: Object creation is centralized as type conditionals, so each new notifier requires modifying existing branch logic
options.explanation: As concrete notifier types increase, this method grows and must be reopened repeatedly. Factory Method structures creation extension more cleanly.

options.text: The key issue is throwing an error for unknown types instead of returning null
options.explanation: Error strategy is secondary; the main concern is creation logic coupled to type branching.

options.text: These classes should all be singletons so creation code is unnecessary
options.explanation: Singletons alter lifecycle semantics but do not solve extensible creation design.

options.text: Using string type values is always wrong and should be replaced by numbers
options.explanation: Identifier representation is not the core architectural issue.

questions.prompt: A framework must support plugin-defined parsers without touching core code each release. What design direction helps most?

options.text: Use a factory method extension point so plugins provide parser creators without editing ParserEngine conditionals
options.explanation: Core engine should rely on parser abstraction while extensions register concrete creators. This avoids constant core modifications.

options.text: Keep adding parser if-branches because parse logic is simple and explicit
options.explanation: Explicit now becomes brittle over time when external plugins and frequent additions are required.

options.text: Switch to reflection that instantiates parser classes from string names directly in parse()
options.explanation: Reflection can hide branch growth but still leaves creation policy in core logic and can reduce safety.

options.text: Use Observer so parsers subscribe to parse requests dynamically
options.explanation: Observer handles event reactions, not deterministic selection and creation of one parser instance.

questions.prompt: Which version becomes harder to maintain as new exporter types are introduced monthly?

options.text: Version A — adding types means reopening conditional creation logic every month, increasing modification risk in shipped code
options.explanation: A grows by branch edits in one method. B delegates concrete creation to subclasses via factory method, reducing churn in core flow.

options.text: Version B — factory methods hide concrete classes, which makes extension less safe than direct constructor calls
options.explanation: Factory methods intentionally hide concrete construction details to make extension safer and more controlled.
